required_env_vars.name: CALCOM_API_KEY
required_env_vars.description: Cal.com V2 API key for fetching coaching session bookings (optional for activity events, required for coaching widget in I3)

tasks.name: Create activity_event_type enum and activity_events table schema
tasks.purpose: Define the enum for event types and the table structure for storing user activity events

constraints: Enum must include: lesson_completed, quiz_completed, survey_completed, course_started, course_completed, presentation_created, presentation_updated
Table columns: id (UUID), user_id (UUID FK), event_type (enum), entity_id (TEXT), entity_type (TEXT), title (TEXT), metadata (JSONB), created_at (TIMESTAMPTZ)
UNIQUE constraint not needed (allow duplicate events for audit trail)

tasks.input_state: No activity_events table or activity_event_type enum exists
tasks.output_state: Schema file exists at apps/web/supabase/schemas/18-activity-events.sql with enum and table definition
tasks.acceptance_criterion: Schema file contains enum definition with 7 event types and table with 8 columns including JSONB metadata

tasks.name: Add RLS policies and indexes to activity_events schema
tasks.purpose: Enable row-level security for user data isolation and add indexes for efficient timeline queries

constraints: RLS policies: SELECT and INSERT only (no UPDATE/DELETE needed for audit trail)
Use (SELECT auth.uid()) = user_id pattern for performance
Indexes: (user_id, created_at DESC) for timeline queries, user_id for RLS filtering

tasks.input_state: Schema file has table definition without RLS or indexes
tasks.output_state: Schema file includes RLS enabled, 2 policies (SELECT, INSERT), and 2 indexes
tasks.acceptance_criterion: Schema file contains ALTER TABLE ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY, 2 CREATE POLICY statements, and 2 CREATE INDEX statements
tasks.verification_command: grep -q 'ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY' apps/web/supabase/schemas/18-activity-events.sql && grep -c 'CREATE POLICY' apps/web/supabase/schemas/18-activity-events.sql | grep -q '^2$' && grep -c 'CREATE INDEX' apps/web/supabase/schemas/18-activity-events.sql | grep -q '^2$'

tasks.name: Create trigger functions for 5 source tables
tasks.purpose: Define PostgreSQL functions that insert activity events when source table records are created or updated

constraints: Function 1: lesson_progress → lesson_completed (when completed_at IS NOT NULL)
Function 2: quiz_attempts → quiz_completed (include score in metadata)
Function 4: course_progress → course_started (INSERT) + course_completed (UPDATE when completed_at IS NOT NULL)
All functions: RETURNS trigger, SET search_path = '', LANGUAGE plpgsql
Denormalize title into activity_events for fast rendering
Use CREATE TRIGGER AFTER INSERT (or AFTER INSERT OR UPDATE for course_progress)

tasks.input_state: Schema file has table and RLS but no trigger functions
tasks.output_state: Schema file includes 5 trigger functions and 5 CREATE TRIGGER statements
tasks.acceptance_criterion: Schema file contains 5 CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION statements and 5 CREATE TRIGGER statements linking to source tables
tasks.verification_command: grep -c 'CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION.*activity_event' apps/web/supabase/schemas/18-activity-events.sql | grep -q '^5$' && grep -c 'CREATE TRIGGER' apps/web/supabase/schemas/18-activity-events.sql | grep -q '^5$'

tasks.name: Generate migration from activity_events schema file
tasks.purpose: Create migration file that applies the schema changes to the database

constraints: Use command: pnpm --filter web supabase:db:diff -f S2045_I1_F2_activity_events
Migration file will be auto-generated in apps/web/supabase/migrations/

tasks.input_state: Schema file complete but no migration file exists
tasks.output_state: Migration file exists in apps/web/supabase/migrations/ with timestamp and name containing 'S2045_I1_F2_activity_events'
tasks.acceptance_criterion: Migration file generated and contains CREATE TYPE, CREATE TABLE, and CREATE TRIGGER statements
